mainText:
Good morning. Here's your Breeze communications briefing for Monday, April 13th.

No new Breeze coverage surfaced over the weekend. Breeze continues to operate without negative press, and local market coverage from recent expansion announcements remains positive.

Now for industry and competitor news.

C.N.B.C. and Bloomberg both reported Friday that A.C.I. Europe, the trade body representing European airports, formally warned that the continent could face a systemic jet fuel shortage within three weeks, as Iran's continued blockade of the Strait of Hormuz has more than doubled jet fuel prices — U.S. carriers United and Delta had already announced capacity cuts and flagged billions in added fuel costs earlier the same week. With domestic jet fuel prices approaching four dollars a gallon, leadership should review Breeze's second and third quarter fuel cost exposure before summer schedules finalize.

Alaska Airlines on Friday became the last major U.S. carrier to raise its checked baggage fees, setting first-bag prices at forty-five dollars and completing an industry-wide wave that now applies to United, Delta, American, Southwest, and Alaska — all citing the Iran war fuel crisis as the driver. With every major competitor now charging forty-five dollars or more for a first bag, Breeze has a clear marketing window to reinforce its value proposition this week.

Southwest Airlines launched its first weekend service from Baltimore to Saint Maarten on Saturday, adding East Coast coverage to the Orlando-to-Saint Maarten service it debuted the prior week. Southwest is now competing directly with Breeze in the East Coast leisure-Caribbean corridor — worth flagging to network planning whether Breeze's Caribbean pricing and messaging require a response.

Weekend operational disruptions hit major U.S. hubs, with Phoenix Sky Harbor logging over one hundred sixty delays on Saturday and United's SkyWest operation at Denver cascading into more than two hundred delays the same day. Breeze's secondary-airport model provides structural protection from hub-contagion disruptions — a differentiator the communications team may want to amplify in consumer messaging.

That's your briefing. Roger is standing by when you're ready to dive deeper or take action on any of these stories.
